content.verdict: The Hyatt Regency Cabo Verde is the newest and most architecturally ambitious 5-star on Sal — a 271-room beachfront resort on Santa Maria with a striking sea-facing main building, multiple pools including a dedicated quiet adults pool, a spa, a kids club, and the standout Miraflores rooftop bar that has become Sal's best sundowner address. For honeymooners, the appeal is the combination of brand-new infrastructure, a calm pool option that buffers couples from family bustle, and a year-round 25°C+ climate at a price that quietly undercuts equivalent product in the Maldives, Seychelles, or Mauritius. Cape Verde sits five hundred kilometres off the West African coast, six hours from London, with a Portuguese-Creole culture (this is Cesária Évora's homeland) that gives the destination a distinct identity — an Africa-adjacent beach holiday that does not feel generic. The Hyatt is the property that pulls all those threads together: international service standards, a polished design language, and the rooftop bar that gives the trip its postcard moment. For a first-time Cape Verde honeymoon that wants the best of the new wave of resorts on the island, this is the lead pick.
content.best_room: The King Bed Ocean View Room is the right starting category — high-floor versions look across the adults pool onto Santa Maria beach and the trade-wind-rippled Atlantic. For honeymooners willing to splurge, the Regency Suite with separate living room and wraparound balcony is the most romantic public-facing room category, and the rare Presidential Suite is the destination's flagship address.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival in Santa Maria
itinerary_7_nights.description: Land at Sal's Amílcar Cabral International Airport (SID), a 15-minute transfer to the Hyatt Regency. Welcome amenity in the ocean-view room, slow afternoon at the adults pool with cocktails. Sundowner at the Miraflores rooftop bar — the best sunset view on Sal — followed by dinner at the resort's main restaurant.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Beach & Adults Pool
itinerary_7_nights.description: First full beach day on Santa Maria's eight-kilometre strip, served from the Hyatt's well-organised beach club. The Atlantic water is genuinely cold (22–24°C) but brilliantly clear; the adults pool is the warmer alternative for a relaxed afternoon. Couples massage at the spa, dinner at the speciality seafood restaurant.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Kitesurfing Introduction
itinerary_7_nights.description: Santa Maria's reliable trade winds and shallow flat water make it one of the world's top kitesurfing spots. Book a half-day intro lesson at one of the schools next door — even non-watersports honeymooners come away buzzing. Lunch back at the Hyatt's pool grill, afternoon recovery at the spa or by the adults pool.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Pedra de Lume Salt Crater
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half-day excursion to the Pedra de Lume volcanic crater, where a hypersaline lake inside an extinct volcano lets you float effortlessly on the surface. The pink salt pans and ochre crater walls are Sal's most distinctive landscape. Stop in Espargos for a local catchupa lunch before returning to the resort.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Desert Quad Biking
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half-day quad-bike adventure across Sal's flat volcanic interior — moonscape lava fields, abandoned salt-mining sites, the Buracona blue eye sea cave on the wild west coast, and quiet northern beaches with no one on them. Late lunch at a local restaurant near Buracona, return to the Hyatt for an early evening at the Miraflores bar.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Catamaran Sunset Cruise
itinerary_7_nights.description: Afternoon catamaran sail from Santa Maria pier with snorkelling stops over the reef, frequent dolphin sightings, and a sundown return as the resort lights pop on along the bay. Dinner ashore at a local seafood restaurant in Santa Maria town — a worthwhile contrast to resort dining.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cesária Évora Music Night
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final beach morning, a long lunch at the pool grill, and an early evening at the Miraflores rooftop. For the last night, head into town for live morna and coladeira at one of the Cesária Évora music bars — the soundtrack of Cape Verde's Portuguese-Creole soul. Late stroll back to the Hyatt along the lit promenade.

caveats: December–February is the Harmattan dust season — Saharan dust can leave skies hazy and the trade winds peak; ideal for kitesurfers, less ideal for sunbathers.
The Atlantic water is cool year-round by tropical standards — fine for swimming, not bathwater warm; the adults pool is the warm-water fallback.
Sal's east coast has documented shark presence; always swim only from the monitored Santa Maria south-coast beach.
Outside the resort zone Sal's infrastructure is basic — Portuguese and Creole dominate, and English is patchy beyond Santa Maria itself.

faqs.question: Is the Hyatt Regency Cabo Verde good for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: Yes — the dedicated adults pool, the Miraflores rooftop bar, the spa, and the polished new-build feel make it one of the strongest couples-focused 5-stars on Sal, even though it accepts families. Honeymooners can comfortably orbit the adults pool and rooftop without ever feeling crowded by the kids-club side of the resort.

faqs.question: Sal or Boavista for a Cape Verde honeymoon?
faqs.answer: Sal has more 5-star choice, better restaurants in Santa Maria town, the strongest kitesurfing, and the Hyatt itself. Boavista is wilder with vast empty beaches and a slower pace. For a first Cape Verde honeymoon focused on convenience and resort quality, Sal — and the Hyatt — is the cleaner pick.

faqs.question: Is the all-inclusive plan worth it at the Hyatt?
faqs.answer: The Hyatt offers room-only, half-board, and all-inclusive rates. Couples who want to eat at the rooftop and explore Santa Maria town will find half-board the sweet spot; couples who want to switch off completely should take the all-inclusive — Cape Verde's outside-resort dining options are limited compared to Caribbean destinations.

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: March through June and October through November are the sweet spots — warm, sunny, with manageable winds. December–February is peak season with the strongest winds and occasional Saharan dust. July–September is hot, humid, with rare short showers.

faqs.question: How long is the flight from London or Europe?
faqs.answer: Around 6 hours direct from London, 4 hours from Lisbon, 6 hours from Amsterdam or Frankfurt. Cape Verde is in the same time zone as the UK in winter, so jet lag is essentially nil — a useful advantage for a short honeymoon.

faqs.question: Is Cape Verde safe for couples?
faqs.answer: Yes — Sal is one of the most relaxed beach destinations in West Africa, malaria-free, with low crime in the resort zone. Standard beach-resort precautions apply (don't leave valuables on the sand, use the in-room safe), but couples regularly walk the Santa Maria promenade in the evening without concern.
